Dear Student,

We are sorry to report that CUSIS was functioning and responding very slowly after the start of the course registration this morning. We had received a lot of enquiries that students could not logon the system successfully. Later, Information Technology Services Centre (ITSC) recommended to shut down CUSIS for trouble-shooting. Therefore we posted an announcement at around 12:35 p.m. that course registration today has to be rescheduled.

This is to inform you that, after consultation with ITSC, the course registration today (6 December 2019) has been rescheduled to 10 December 2019, also from 10:00 a.m. to 10:00 p.m. Please check your revised time-appointment for course registration under CUSIS. For some students who might have successfully registered some courses this morning, they are reminded to check whether their course enrollment was successful.

Both ITSC and RES would like to send our apology for the inconvenience caused.

Regards,
Registration and Examinations Section
6 December 2019

香港中文大學（中大）有一名學生離港後於12月29日返港，於1月4日感到身體不適，今天（1月5日）早上返宿舍後，曾外出往私家醫院求診，及後自行前往深圳的醫院跟進檢查。

該同學傍晚回到宿舍，在救護人員陪同下，她和其同房宿生一同前往公立醫院作進一步檢驗。

大學隨即按照衛生防護中心的指引，以漂白水清潔該學生的房間、居住樓層及公眾地方，以保障宿生健康，亦將衞生防護中心的健康指引張貼在宿舍告示板供同學參考。該宿舍並會提供手術口罩、酒精搓手液給宿生使用。若同學感到不適或有顧慮，可前往大學保健處進行檢查。
